behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git rename detection plus CLI categorization. A common real failure is invoking diff/status without rename detection (or with wrong flags), which makes renames appear as add+delete. MOCK-MISS: this is not only local logic; it is module handoff from Git output to report aggregation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked diff lines can hide command/flag/output-shape bugs. Rejection gate: Unit/Integration are rejected because the behavior semantically depends on how a real repository change is surfaced by Git, not just on deterministic parser input.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit a file, rename it with content similarity preserved, run the CLI, and assert: renamed count increments, added/deleted counts do not include that file, and stdout report places it only in renamed.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode depends on real binary diff behavior from Git and process output handling (encoding/content edge cases). REAL-INFRA: real repo + real Git diff output is needed. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing text diff output can miss crashes caused by real binary metadata/output forms. Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because semantic failure is tied to actual binary diff handling in real Git I/O, not only internal counting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real repo with a committed binary file and a binary change, run the CLI, assert process exit success, no traceback on stderr, and binary change is included in the appropriate count/category in stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets real Git empty-repo semantics (unborn HEAD, command exit codes/messages) and CLI error handling. REAL-INFRA: needs real Git repository state with zero commits. MOCK-HIDE: fake repository responses can miss actual subprocess failure modes. Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because the failure semantics are about real VCS/CLI interaction, not just internal branching.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real Git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit code and a valid empty structured report on stdout (no fatal error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, requiring real commit graph/diff behavior and CLI execution path. MOCK-MISS: several-module interaction (history reader, classifier, aggregator, formatter). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic fixtures can miss command-range, rename heuristics, and history traversal mismatches seen only with real Git state. Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because the behavior explicitly ties correctness to the real repository history, not just in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a controlled sequence of commits covering add/delete/modify/rename, run the CLI over the target range, and assert reported category counts equal independently verified counts from Git commands for the same range.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is Git index vs working-tree behavior, which is an OS/filesystem-backed repository state distinction exposed through real Git plumbing/porcelain calls. REAL-INFRA: requires actual staged and unstaged states in a real repo. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can force equivalence and miss command selection/path bugs (e.g., comparing HEAD..index vs index..worktree inconsistently). Rejection gate: Integration is rejected because semantic failure depends on real staging mechanics, not just local transformation logic.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent content changes once as staged and once as unstaged (in controlled runs), run the CLI in both cases, normalize ordering if report contract requires, and assert stdout structured output is identical.
